title: Starbucks is paying baristas to make TikToks. Expect more brands to follow.
tldr: Starbucks is compensating frontline employees to create TikTok content, signaling a scaled, formalized approach to employee-generated content as a brand channel. A likely template for other brands.
angle: When brands put employees on payroll for content: the governance, authenticity and pay questions behind formalized EGC.

title: Unilever CMO: Marketing has become 'efficient in creating things people ignore'
tldr: Unilever's CMO argues marketing has optimized for volume over attention, producing content audiences tune out. A sharp critique of efficiency-driven content culture.
angle: Efficiency vs. attention: why more content is making brands more ignorable, and what human-first output looks like.

title: Hot Ones creator Sean Evans on YouTube vs. TV, the interview boom and what comes next
tldr: The Hot Ones host reflects on the creator economy's evolution, the interview-format boom, and the shifting YouTube-vs-TV balance. A concrete practitioner view on format trends.
angle: The interview-format boom: why long-form personality-driven talk beats polished TV, and lessons for brand content.

title: In Graphic Detail: The state of streaming highlights the power of creators
tldr: 'Just Chatting' and personality-driven creators now drive views on major streaming platforms, showing how media consumption is shifting toward informal human presence over produced content.
angle: Why 'just chatting' wins: the rise of unpolished, presence-based content and what it says about audience psychology.

title: Stop asking employees to adopt AI
tldr: McKinsey data shows employees already use generative AI 3x more than leaders realize, yet only 1% of companies have integrated it — reframing AI adoption as a change-management problem, not a mandate.
why_fresh: reframes AI adoption gap with data
angle: The AI adoption gap is a comms problem: how internal communications, not mandates, drives real change.

title: Elena Verna Says AI Confidence Theater Is Eroding Trust in Every Brand. She's Right.
tldr: Argues that AI-generated 'confidence theater' — polished but hollow output — is undermining brand trust. A pointed take on authenticity in the AI content era.
why_fresh: new framing of AI trust erosion
angle: 'Confidence theater': why AI polish erodes trust and how human-centered content rebuilds it.

title: 5 Signs Your Financial Content Program Has a Credibility Problem
tldr: AI engines and buyers now reward named, credentialed experts over anonymous brand content. Five diagnostic signs and fixes for credibility gaps.
angle: Named experts over brand bylines: how AI search is forcing content programs to put humans front and center.

title: Workato's First Thought Leadership Hire Connects Content Directly to Pipeline
tldr: Case study of a B2B company's first dedicated thought-leadership hire tying editorial output directly to revenue pipeline — a concrete model for content-to-business attribution.
angle: Making thought leadership accountable: structuring the role so content ties to pipeline, not vanity.

title: My Job Changed and Nobody Told Me: What AI Visibility Means for Marketers
tldr: AI visibility (being cited by answer engines) has quietly redefined the roles of comms and marketing pros across paid, earned, owned and shared media. A practical framing of the shift.
angle: AI visibility as the new share of voice: how PESO strategy shifts when answer engines are the audience.

title: Fact-checkers reckon with shrinking budgets and growing AI threats at GlobalFact 2026
tldr: At GlobalFact 2026 in Vilnius, fact-checkers confronted declining grant funding while AI-generated disinformation surges — a squeeze with major implications for information integrity.
why_fresh: fresh conference reporting on twin crisis
angle: When disinfo scales and fact-checking shrinks: what the funding gap means for brand and corporate reputation.

title: Rachel Many's posters highlight how creative mothers are paying twice for an industry in freefall
tldr: An LA designer turns the economics of motherhood in creative work into data-backed visual essays — a strong data-storytelling and info-design case study.
why_fresh: data-driven visual storytelling on human issue
angle: Turning hard numbers into human-centered posters: a masterclass in data storytelling that provokes.

title: Rampiq Reveals What Content AI Search Engines Cite Most In B2B
tldr: New research breaks down which content types and formats AI answer engines cite most in B2B — practical guidance for optimizing for LLM visibility.
why_fresh: new data on AI citation patterns
angle: Writing for the machines that answer: what B2B content actually gets cited by LLMs.

title: Americans of all ages are spending less time socializing
tldr: New Time Use Survey data shows socializing has fallen from 45 to 35 minutes a day across every generation — a structural shift in how people spend attention and connect.
why_fresh: fresh behavioral data with content implications
angle: The socializing recession and the attention economy: what declining human contact means for how content lands.

title: Connected Content: Why Omnicom Says Traditional Advertising No Longer Matches Consumer Expectations
tldr: Omnicom argues traditional advertising is out of step with how consumers now expect connected, contextual content — a strategic reframe from a major holding company.
angle: Connected content vs. interruption advertising: matching output to modern consumption expectations.
